Step Action: Inspect all site images using inspection tools or screen reader
Step Expected: Each image must have an alt attribute with meaningful description or alt='' if decorative

Step Action: Verify functional images (buttons, links) with accessibility inspector
Step Expected: Functional images must describe the action or destination, not just the visual image

Step Action: Review complex images (charts, diagrams) and look for extended descriptions
Step Expected: Complex images must have accessible long description via longdesc, aria-describedby, or adjacent text

Title: TC - [A11y] - 1.2 - Time-based Media - 1.2.1 - Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)

Step Action: Play audio-only or video-only content and verify available alternatives
Step Expected: A text transcript or accessible alternative description must exist alongside the content

Step Action: Verify that transcript includes speaker identification and relevant sounds
Step Expected: Transcript must be complete, identify who is speaking, and describe important sounds for comprehension

Step Action: Play prerecorded videos on the site
Step Expected: Videos must include synchronized activatable captions covering all dialogue and relevant sounds

Step Action: Verify caption synchronization and accuracy with audio
Step Expected: Captions must appear synchronized with audio, without significant errors, covering relevant sound effects

Title: TC - [A11y] - 1.2 - Time-based Media - 1.2.3 - Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)

Step Action: Play videos with essential visual content and verify descriptions
Step Expected: Audio description or text alternative must exist describing important visual information

Step Action: Verify that alternative covers all critical visual elements for comprehension
Step Expected: Description must include actions, expressions, scene changes, and unspoken on-screen text

Step Action: Access live broadcasts if available
Step Expected: Live broadcasts must have real-time captioning

Step Action: Verify accuracy and acceptable delay of live captions
Step Expected: Captions must reflect spoken audio with reasonable accuracy and minimal delay (maximum 3-5 seconds)

Step Action: Identify prerecorded videos containing essential visual information not conveyed in audio
Step Expected: Videos must include audio description covering actions, characters, scene changes, and relevant visual elements

Step Action: Play video with audio description enabled and verify synchronization
Step Expected: Audio description must be inserted in natural pauses without overlapping main audio

Step Action: Verify that audio description covers all critical visual elements
Step Expected: Essential visual information must be fully described for blind or low vision users

Step Action: Identify prerecorded audio content (videos with narration or dialogue)
Step Expected: Videos must include sign language interpretation visible on screen or activatable

Step Action: Verify visibility and size of sign language interpreter
Step Expected: Interpreter must be clearly visible, with adequate contrast and sufficient size to distinguish signs

Step Action: Verify synchronization between audio and sign language
Step Expected: Sign interpretation must be synchronized with spoken audio without significant delays

Title: TC - [A11y] - 1.2 - Time-based Media - 1.2.7 - Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)

Step Action: Identify videos where natural pauses are insufficient for audio description
Step Expected: Video must automatically pause to allow extended audio description of complex visual information

Step Action: Play video with extended audio description and verify pauses
Step Expected: Video must pause when needed to describe visual elements, then continue automatically

Step Action: Verify user control over extended audio description
Step Expected: User must be able to enable or disable extended audio description per preference

Step Action: Confirm that extended description does not interfere with content comprehension
Step Expected: Pauses and descriptions must integrate naturally without causing confusion or loss of narrative context

Step Action: Locate prerecorded videos and verify availability of complete text document
Step Expected: A complete text transcript must exist including dialogues, visual descriptions, and relevant sounds

Step Action: Review that text alternative describes complete sequence of visual events
Step Expected: Document must function as independent alternative, describing all essential visual information chronologically

Step Action: Verify accessibility of text alternative
Step Expected: Transcript must be in accessible format (HTML, structured document) near the original video

Step Action: Access live audio-only broadcast (podcast, web radio, conference)
Step Expected: An equivalent alternative must be presented synchronized with the live audio

Step Action: Verify delivery method of synchronized alternative
Step Expected: Alternative can be real-time transcript, synchronized text description, or updated equivalent content

Step Action: Verify that alternative captures essential information from live audio
Step Expected: Text content must reflect critical audio information with minimal acceptable delay

Step Action: Inspect HTML code of tables, lists, and headings with DevTools
Step Expected: Tables use thead/tbody/th with scope, lists use ul/ol/li, headings use h1-h6 hierarchically

Step Action: Verify forms with programmatically associated labels
Step Expected: Each input must have label with for attribute or be wrapped in label, visible to screen readers

Step Action: Review related field groups with fieldset and legend
Step Expected: Control groups (radio buttons, checkboxes) must be grouped with fieldset and descriptive legend

Step Action: Navigate the site using only Tab and verify reading order
Step Expected: Tab and DOM order must match the logical visual order of content

Step Action: Use screen reader to verify content reading order
Step Expected: Reading order must be logical and coherent, without confusing jumps caused by CSS positioning

Step Action: Identify instructions that use only shape, color, or position
Step Expected: Instructions must include descriptive text in addition to visual references (e.g., 'green Submit button' not just 'green button')

Step Action: Verify that critical information does not rely solely on sensory elements
Step Expected: Errors, alerts, or process steps must be communicated with text in addition to color, icons, or sounds

Step Action: Rotate device or change browser orientation
Step Expected: Content must work in portrait and landscape orientation without loss of functionality

Step Action: Verify that content is not locked to a single orientation
Step Expected: Application must not force specific orientation unless essential (e.g., piano app, bank check)

Step Action: Inspect form fields in source code
Step Expected: Common fields (name, email, phone) must have appropriate autocomplete attribute

Step Action: Test autocomplete with browser saved data
Step Expected: Fields with correct autocomplete must suggest user saved values when typing begins

Step Action: Review complex UI components and verify programmatic purpose
Step Expected: Components must identify their purpose through ARIA attributes or HTML semantics for assistive technologies

Step Action: Verify icons and interactive elements with accessibility inspector
Step Expected: Buttons, links, and icons must expose their function through appropriate roles and accessible names

Step Action: Identify elements where color conveys information (errors, states, links)
Step Expected: Information conveyed by color must include additional indicator (text, icon, pattern)

Step Action: Test site in grayscale mode or with color blindness simulator
Step Expected: All information must remain comprehensible without relying on color to distinguish elements

Step Action: Access pages with auto-playing audio
Step Expected: A visible control must exist to pause, stop, or mute audio playing for more than 3 seconds

Step Action: Verify that audio control is keyboard accessible
Step Expected: Control must be operable without mouse, using Tab and Enter/Space

Step Action: Measure text contrast with tool (e.g., Colour Contrast Analyser)
Step Expected: Normal text: minimum 4.5:1, large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold+): minimum 3:1

Step Action: Verify contrast in all states (normal, hover, focus, disabled)
Step Expected: All interactive element states must meet required contrast ratios

Step Action: Review text over images or gradient backgrounds
Step Expected: Overlaid text must have sufficient contrast throughout or use shadows/overlays

Step Action: Zoom to 200% in browser
Step Expected: Text must be readable and content accessible without horizontal scroll or overlaps

Step Action: Verify that controls and buttons remain operable when zoomed
Step Expected: Interactive elements must not be clipped or hidden when text size is increased

Step Action: Identify text rendered as image
Step Expected: Text must be in real HTML, not images (except logos or special cases)

Step Action: Inspect graphics and banners for embedded text
Step Expected: Titles, labels, and informative text must be CSS-styled HTML text, not images

Step Action: Measure text contrast in critical elements with analysis tools
Step Expected: Normal text: minimum 7:1, large text: minimum 4.5:1 for AAA compliance

Step Action: Document exceptions and plan contrast improvements
Step Expected: Identify areas not meeting AAA and prioritize changes in most critical elements

Title: TC - [A11y] - 1.4 - Distinguishable - 1.4.7 - Low or No Background Audio

Step Action: Play audio content and evaluate background sound presence
Step Expected: Background audio must be removable, 20dB lower than main audio, or non-existent

Step Action: Verify control to disable background music if present
Step Expected: User must be able to mute or reduce ambient audio without affecting main narration

Step Action: Verify if user can customize spacing, column width, alignment, and fonts
Step Expected: System must allow visual presentation adjustments without loss of functionality or content

Step Action: Test readability with OS settings (text size, high contrast)
Step Expected: Content must respect user preferences defined at system level

Title: TC - [A11y] - 1.4 - Distinguishable - 1.4.9 - Images of Text (No Exception)

Step Action: Thoroughly review entire site for text rendered as images
Step Expected: No text in image format must exist, all must be real HTML text (no exceptions except logos)

Step Action: Inspect decorative elements and verify text is styled with CSS
Step Expected: Visual text effects must be achieved with CSS, not by converting text to images

Step Action: Resize window to 320px width or zoom to 400%
Step Expected: Horizontal scrolling must not be required to read content (except complex tables)

Step Action: Test on real mobile device with narrow viewport
Step Expected: All content must adapt fluidly without clipping or need for bidirectional scrolling

Step Action: Measure contrast of UI components and focus states
Step Expected: Minimum 3:1 contrast for component borders, functional icons, and focus indicators

Step Action: Verify contrast of informative graphic elements (charts, status icons)
Step Expected: Visual elements conveying information must be clearly distinguishable from background

Step Action: Apply increased spacing CSS (line-height: 1.5, letter-spacing: 0.12em, etc.)
Step Expected: Content must remain readable and functional without clipping or overlaps

Step Action: Verify that buttons and controls do not break with modified spacing
Step Expected: Interactive elements must maintain functionality and readability with custom spacing

Title: TC - [A11y] - 1.4 - Distinguishable - 1.4.13 - Content on Hover or Focus

Step Action: Activate tooltips and popup content with hover and keyboard
Step Expected: Additional content must be keyboard accessible, remain visible, and be dismissible without moving pointer

Step Action: Verify that popup content does not disappear when moving pointer over it
Step Expected: User must be able to move cursor over tooltip to read or interact without it disappearing

Step Action: Verify accessible dismiss mechanism (ESC or close button)
Step Expected: Popup content must be dismissible without moving pointer from the trigger that activated it

Step Action: Navigate entire site using only keyboard (Tab, Enter, Space, arrows)
Step Expected: All functions must be operable: menus, modals, forms, interactive controls

Step Action: Test complex components (accordions, tabs, calendars)
Step Expected: Custom widgets must respond to appropriate keys (arrows, Home, End, Escape)

Step Action: Verify all mouse actions have keyboard equivalent
Step Expected: Drag and drop, hover menus, touch gestures must have keyboard-operable alternatives

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.1 - Keyboard Accessible - 2.1.2 - No Keyboard Trap

Step Action: Navigate with Tab through all components
Step Expected: User must be able to exit any component using only keyboard (Tab, Shift+Tab, Esc)

Step Action: Test modals, dialogs, and overlays with keyboard navigation
Step Expected: Focus must move freely within component and exit without being trapped

Step Action: Verify that absolutely all functions are keyboard operable
Step Expected: No function should require mouse or gestures, all must have keyboard equivalent

Step Action: Document functions requiring specific pointer path
Step Expected: If function requires specific path (signature, drawing), keyboard alternative or alternative method must exist

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.1 - Keyboard Accessible - 2.1.4 - Character Key Shortcuts

Step Action: Test single character keyboard shortcuts if they exist
Step Expected: Shortcuts must be disableable, remappable, or only work when component has focus

Step Action: Verify shortcuts do not interfere with screen readers or assistive tools
Step Expected: Key combinations must avoid conflicts with assistive technology commands

Step Action: Identify timers or sessions with time limits
Step Expected: User must be able to extend, adjust, or disable time limits before expiration

Step Action: Verify prior warning before time expires
Step Expected: System must alert with sufficient advance notice and allow simple extension (e.g., press button)

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.2 - Enough Time - 2.2.2 - Pause, Stop, Hide

Step Action: Identify auto-moving content (carousels, animations)
Step Expected: Accessible control must exist to pause, stop, or hide content moving for more than 5 seconds

Step Action: Verify pause control is visible and keyboard operable
Step Expected: Pause/play buttons must be clearly visible and accessible without mouse

Step Action: Review all processes and verify existence of timeouts
Step Expected: No time limits should exist in any interaction, or timeless alternatives must exist

Step Action: Document exceptions where time is a real-time requirement
Step Expected: Identify live events or auctions where time limit is essential and cannot be removed

Step Action: Test system interruptions (notifications, automatic alerts)
Step Expected: User must be able to postpone or suppress interruptions except emergencies

Step Action: Verify notification preferences settings
Step Expected: System must offer controls to manage frequency and type of interruptions

Step Action: Let session expire and re-authenticate
Step Expected: Entered data must be preserved after re-authentication without information loss

Step Action: Test with long forms and verify data recovery
Step Expected: In-progress information must be auto-saved or recoverable after relogin

Step Action: Identify session timeouts and verify handling
Step Expected: System must warn about timeouts with sufficient notice and allow data saving

Step Action: Verify user can save work before timeout
Step Expected: Warning must include clear option to save progress or extend session

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.3 - Seizures and Physical Reactions - 2.3.1 - Three Flashes or Below Threshold

Step Action: Review site animations and transitions
Step Expected: No content should flash more than 3 times per second

Step Action: Use PEAT (Photosensitive Epilepsy Analysis Tool) if available
Step Expected: Analyze multimedia content to identify flashes that could cause seizures

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.3 - Seizures and Physical Reactions - 2.3.2 - Three Flashes

Step Action: Thoroughly analyze animations, videos, and dynamic content
Step Expected: No flash exceeding safe limits must exist, without exceptions

Step Action: Review embedded third-party content (ads, external videos)
Step Expected: External content must not contain dangerous flashes or must be disableable

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.3 - Seizures and Physical Reactions - 2.3.3 - Animation from Interactions

Step Action: Verify motion animations triggered by scroll or interaction
Step Expected: User must be able to disable non-essential motion animations

Step Action: Verify respect for system prefers-reduced-motion preference
Step Expected: Site must detect and respect OS setting to reduce motion

Step Action: Navigate with keyboard from page start
Step Expected: A 'Skip to main content' link must be visible on Tab press or appropriate ARIA landmarks

Step Action: Verify ARIA landmarks (main, nav, complementary, contentinfo)
Step Expected: Structure must use landmark roles to enable quick navigation with screen readers

Step Action: Review <title> element on each site page
Step Expected: Each page must have a unique descriptive title identifying its content

Step Action: Verify title follows consistent format (content - section - site name)
Step Expected: Titles must follow predictable pattern facilitating identification in tabs and history

Step Action: Navigate with Tab in order and verify visual logic
Step Expected: Focus order must follow logical sequence left to right, top to bottom

Step Action: Identify elements with positive tabindex and evaluate necessity
Step Expected: Avoid tabindex greater than 0, order must be controlled through logical DOM structure

Step Action: Review all site links
Step Expected: Link text or surrounding context must clearly describe its destination (avoid 'click here')

Step Action: Use screen reader to list all links
Step Expected: Links must be understandable when presented out of context in link list

Step Action: Try to locate an internal page in different ways
Step Expected: Navigation menu, internal search, sitemap, or breadcrumbs must exist

Step Action: Verify at least two navigation methods are available
Step Expected: User must be able to choose between different strategies to locate content

Step Action: Inspect headings (h1-h6) and form labels
Step Expected: Headings must clearly describe sections, labels must identify field purpose

Step Action: Use screen reader to navigate by headings
Step Expected: Heading structure must allow quick understanding of content organization

Step Action: Navigate with Tab and observe focus indicator
Step Expected: Interactive elements must show clear contrasted visual indicator when focused

Step Action: Verify outline is not disabled without visual replacement
Step Expected: If default outline is removed, equally visible alternative indicator must exist

Step Action: Navigate through different site sections and verify location indicators
Step Expected: Breadcrumbs, section titles, or indicators showing user location must exist

Step Action: Review that breadcrumbs are accessible and reflect correct hierarchy
Step Expected: Breadcrumbs must be keyboard navigable and structured with ordered list

Step Action: Review link text in isolation, without surrounding context
Step Expected: Each link must be understandable by itself without need for additional context

Step Action: Identify generic links ('more info', 'read more') and improve them
Step Expected: Ambiguous links must include additional context via aria-label or aria-describedby

Step Action: Review content structure and heading usage
Step Expected: Content must be organized with descriptive headings dividing information into logical sections

Step Action: Verify no long text blocks exist without intermediate headings
Step Expected: Extensive content must be subdivided with headings facilitating scanning and navigation

Step Action: Navigate with keyboard and verify focus visibility on elements near edges
Step Expected: At least part of focus indicator must remain visible, not completely hidden by other elements

Step Action: Test navigation in areas with sticky headers or floating elements
Step Expected: Sticky bars must not completely cover the focused element

Step Action: Verify focus is completely visible in all situations
Step Expected: Focus indicator must be completely visible without any obstruction by other elements

Step Action: Implement auto-scroll when element receives focus outside viewport
Step Expected: Content must auto-adjust to fully show the focused element

Step Action: Measure focus indicator area and contrast with specialized tools
Step Expected: Indicator must have sufficient minimum area and high contrast for maximum visibility

Step Action: Calculate that indicator has minimum 2 CSS pixels perimeter or equivalent area
Step Expected: Indicator thickness and area must meet specific WCAG 2.2 requirements

Step Action: Identify functions activated by complex gestures (swipe, pinch)
Step Expected: Functions must have alternative with simple single-point gestures (tap, click)

Step Action: Verify carousels, maps, and swipeable components
Step Expected: Alternative buttons or controls must exist for users with motor limitations

Step Action: Test activation of interactive elements
Step Expected: Function must not execute on mousedown/touchstart, must allow cancellation by releasing outside target

Step Action: Verify accidental clicks can be cancelled by dragging outside
Step Expected: User must be able to undo accidental activation by moving pointer before release

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.5 - Input Modalities - 2.5.3 - Label in Name

Step Action: Inspect controls with visible labels using screen reader
Step Expected: Accessible name of control must include text of its visible label

Step Action: Verify voice commands can activate controls using visible text
Step Expected: Voice control users must be able to say visible text to activate element

Step Action: Identify functions using accelerometer or gyroscope
Step Expected: Functions activated by device motion must have standard UI alternative

Step Action: Verify setting to disable motion actuation
Step Expected: User must be able to disable motion responses to avoid accidental activations

Title: TC - [A11y] - 2.5 - Input Modalities - 2.5.6 - Concurrent Input Mechanisms

Step Action: Test site with multiple simultaneous input devices (keyboard + mouse + touch)
Step Expected: Site must support simultaneous use of different input mechanisms without restrictions

Step Action: Verify switching input method does not cause loss of functionality
Step Expected: User must be able to freely switch between keyboard, mouse, and touch without issues

Step Action: Identify drag and drop functions
Step Expected: Drag and drop functions must have alternative with simple clicks or taps

Step Action: Test keyboard alternatives for reordering elements
Step Expected: Reorderable lists must be manipulable with keyboard (arrows + Space/Enter)

Step Action: Measure dimensions of touch controls (buttons, links)
Step Expected: Touch targets must have minimum 24x24 CSS pixels or equivalent spacing

Step Action: Verify spacing between adjacent touch targets
Step Expected: If targets are smaller than 24px, sufficient surrounding space must exist to avoid errors

Step Action: Inspect lang attribute on <html> tag
Step Expected: Lang attribute must be present and specify correct language of main content

Step Action: Verify correct pronunciation with screen reader
Step Expected: Reader must use voice and pronunciation rules appropriate to declared language

Step Action: Identify text fragments in language different from main
Step Expected: Elements with language change must have appropriate lang attribute

Step Action: Test screen reader reading of quotes or terms in another language
Step Expected: Language changes must be recognized and pronounced correctly

Step Action: Identify jargon, technical terms, or unusual words in content
Step Expected: Glossary, inline definitions, or mechanism to explain uncommon terms must exist

Step Action: Verify accessibility of definitions through links or dfn/abbr elements
Step Expected: Specialized terms must link to accessible explanations

Step Action: Locate abbreviations and acronyms in content
Step Expected: First instance of abbreviations must include full expansion or link to definition

Step Action: Implement abbr element with title attribute where appropriate
Step Expected: Abbreviations must be semantically marked for assistive technologies

Step Action: Evaluate text complexity and required reading level
Step Expected: Complex content must have simplified version or summary for lower secondary education level

Step Action: Use readability analysis tools (Flesch-Kincaid, INFLESZ)
Step Expected: Measure readability indices and provide alternatives for technical or complex content

Step Action: Identify words where pronunciation is critical for comprehension
Step Expected: Mechanism to indicate pronunciation must exist (IPA, audio, phonetic text)

Step Action: Verify homographs and ambiguous terms with pronunciation guides
Step Expected: Words with multiple pronunciations must be clarified when context is insufficient

Step Action: Navigate with Tab through interactive elements
Step Expected: No element should cause context change (navigation, submission) just by receiving focus

Step Action: Verify form fields do not submit when focused
Step Expected: Focus must be independent of activation, changes must only occur with explicit action

Step Action: Interact with forms and controls without activating submit button
Step Expected: Field changes must not cause context change without prior warning or confirmation

Step Action: Test selects and radios that may cause automatic navigation
Step Expected: If change causes action, prior warning or confirmation button must be required

Step Action: Compare menus and navigation across different pages
Step Expected: Repeated navigation elements must appear in same order across all pages

Step Action: Document location of global elements (search, login, main menu)
Step Expected: Common components must maintain consistent position and order throughout site

Step Action: Identify components with same function across different pages
Step Expected: Equivalent components must use same labels, icons, and accessible names

Step Action: Verify consistency of icons and symbols throughout site
Step Expected: Same icon must represent same function, avoid different symbols for identical actions

Step Action: Verify significant changes require explicit user action
Step Expected: Context changes, submissions, or navigation must not occur without explicit confirmation

Step Action: Identify and fix automatic submissions or unexpected redirects
Step Expected: User must initiate all important changes through clear buttons or links

Step Action: Search for help mechanisms in different sections
Step Expected: Help options must appear in consistent location throughout site

Step Action: Verify quick access to contact, FAQ, chat, or documentation
Step Expected: Help must be available predictably in fixed location

Step Action: Submit form with intentional errors
Step Expected: Errors must be clearly identified in text, visually marked, and announced to screen readers

Step Action: Verify error messages use aria-describedby or aria-invalid
Step Expected: Errors must be programmatically associated with affected fields for assistive technologies

Step Action: Verify focus moves to first error or error summary
Step Expected: After validation, user must be clearly directed to found problems

Title: TC - [A11y] - 3.3 - Input Assistance - 3.3.2 - Labels or Instructions

Step Action: Review all form fields
Step Expected: Each field must have visible and programmatically associated label, with clear instructions if needed

Step Action: Verify fields with specific formats (date, phone, zip code)
Step Expected: Fields with format requirements must include examples or input masks

Step Action: Trigger validation errors in forms
Step Expected: Error messages must suggest how to fix the specific problem

Step Action: Evaluate message quality: must be specific and solution-oriented
Step Expected: Errors must explain what is wrong and how to fix it, not just indicate there is an error

Title: TC - [A11y] - 3.3 - Input Assistance - 3.3.4 - Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data)

Step Action: Complete financial or legal transactions if they exist
Step Expected: Must allow reviewing, confirming, and correcting information before final submission

Step Action: Verify confirmation page with option to edit before final submission
Step Expected: Critical data must be shown in summary with ability to go back and modify

Step Action: Search for contextual help in forms and complex tasks
Step Expected: Available help must exist to complete tasks, visible or easily accessible

Step Action: Verify tooltips, help links, or info icons next to complex fields
Step Expected: Help must be available in context where needed, not only on separate page

Step Action: Identify forms that modify, delete, or compromise user data
Step Expected: Reversible mechanism (undo), verification (review before submit), or explicit confirmation must exist

Step Action: Test form submission with critical information
Step Expected: System must present review screen where user can verify and correct before confirming

Step Action: Verify availability of option to revert completed actions
Step Expected: Destructive actions must allow undo or have grace period before permanent execution

Step Action: Verify explicit confirmations for irreversible actions
Step Expected: Deletions, transfers, or permanent changes must require clear and specific additional confirmation

Step Action: Complete multi-step forms
Step Expected: Previously provided information must auto-complete or be selectable, not re-entered

Step Action: Verify data from previous steps is pre-filled in later steps
Step Expected: User must not re-type name, address, or other already entered data

Step Action: Test login and authentication process
Step Expected: Must offer accessible alternatives to visual CAPTCHA or cognitive tests (email, SMS, biometrics)

Step Action: Verify availability of magic link or OTP code authentication
Step Expected: Alternative methods must be equally secure without requiring visual recognition

Step Action: Evaluate all available authentication methods
Step Expected: System must offer methods not relying on object recognition or complex cognitive tests

Step Action: Document and promote use of biometric authentication or password managers
Step Expected: Authentication options must include AAA methods not requiring complex memory

Step Action: Validate HTML with W3C Validator or similar tool
Step Expected: No critical errors must exist: duplicate IDs, improperly nested tags, missing required attributes

Step Action: Review dynamically generated code with browser inspector
Step Expected: JavaScript generating content must produce valid and well-formed HTML

Step Action: Inspect custom controls with accessibility inspector
Step Expected: All controls must expose correct name, role, and value to accessibility API

Step Action: Test custom widgets with screen reader
Step Expected: Components must announce correctly with appropriate ARIA role, state, and properties

Step Action: Verify state changes are reflected in ARIA attributes
Step Expected: Dynamic states (expanded, selected, pressed) must be updated programmatically

Step Action: Trigger dynamic messages (confirmation, error, loading)
Step Expected: Messages must be announced in screen readers using appropriate ARIA live regions

Step Action: Verify correct usage of role=alert, aria-live=polite/assertive
Step Expected: Critical messages use assertive, informational use polite, without moving focus

Step Action: Test with screen reader that announcements interrupt or wait appropriately
Step Expected: Live regions must be configured by message urgency without causing excessive interruptions
